error_signature: Input cannot be null or undefined.
title: Input data is null or undefined in Code node
symptom: The Code node throws an error stating 'Input cannot be null or undefined.' when trying to parse email content.
applies_when: Using SimpleParser in a Code node
Input data is expected to contain email content
not_this_if: Input data contains the expected property
root_cause: This error occurs when the input to the SimpleParser does not contain the expected property, leading to a null or undefined value being passed. In this case, the code is trying to access 'data' instead of the correct property 'emlText'.
fix_steps: Check the input structure of the previous node to confirm the correct property name.
Update the code to access the correct property, changing 'const emailContent = $input.first().json['data'];' to 'const emailContent = $input.first().json['emlText'];'.
agent_instruction: Ensure you are accessing the correct property from the input data in your Code node.
